zh.lifeSummary: Jane ferch Hywel Or Howell x於大約1270年出生在威爾士安格萊。她和Hywel ap Iorwerth Fychan ap Iorwerth ap Gwarin ap Caradog ap Ynyr Fychan至少有3個兒子和2個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jane ferch Hywel Or Howell x于大约1270年出生在威尔士安格萊。她和Hywel ap Iorwerth Fychan ap Iorwerth ap Gwarin ap Caradog ap Ynyr Fychan至少有3个儿子和2个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

variants.name: Ferg
meanings: German: occupational name for a ferryman, Middle High German verje, verge.

nameMeanings.name: Hywel
meanings: A Welsh name. Some write the name Hoel, which they render 'lordly;' if so, it may come from huail, a vice-regent, regent. Arthur derives the surname Howell from Corn. -Brit, houl, the sun; and he adds, high, exalted.' But Hywel is rather from hywel, conspicuous.

nameMeanings.name: Or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Howell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh and English: in the Welsh border counties, from the Welsh male personal name Hywel ‘eminent’, popular since the Middle Ages particularly in honor of the great 10th-century law-giving Welsh king. The name may also arise from the Breton personal name Houuel, Huwel, Huwal, Howael, cognate with the Welsh name. Bretons were especially numerous in the eastern counties of England. See Powell .
English: perhaps from a Middle English personal name Huwel (spelled Howel), a shortening of Huwelot or Huelin (see Hewlett and Huling ), both pet forms of Middle English Huw (see Hugh ).
English: habitational name from Howell (Lincolnshire), from an uncertain initial element + Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’.

variants.name: Howle
meanings: English and Welsh:
variant of   Howell  .
variant of   Hole  .

variants.name: Howells
meanings: English and Welsh (Glamorgan): variant of Howell , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent English -s.

English: perhaps occasionally a nickname for someone who was fearless, from Middle English houe, houwe ‘anxiety, fear’ (Old English hogu or hoga) + les ‘less’. If this has survived as a modern surname, its pronunciation has been influenced by that of the name in 1 above.
English: variant of   Holes  .

variants.name: Powell
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Hywel ‘son of Hywel’, a personal name meaning ‘eminent’ (see Howell ).
Irish: mainly of Welsh origin as in 1 above, but sometimes a surname adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Giolla Phóil ‘son of the servant of Saint Paul’ (see Guilfoyle ).
English: variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jane ferch Hywel Or Howell x was born about 1270, in Anglesey, Wales. She had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Hywel ap Iorwerth Fychan ap Iorwerth ap Gwarin ap Caradog ap Ynyr Fychan.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane ferch Hywel Or Howell x est née environ 1270, à Anglesey, Galles. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Hywel ap Iorwerth Fychan ap Iorwerth ap Gwarin ap Caradog ap Ynyr Fychan.

de.lifeSummary: Jane ferch Hywel Or Howell x wurde um 1270 in Anglesey, Wales geboren. Sie hatte mit Hywel ap Iorwerth Fychan ap Iorwerth ap Gwarin ap Caradog ap Ynyr Fychan mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Jane ferch Hywel Or Howell x è nata il circa 1270 nel Anglesey, Galles. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Hywel ap Iorwerth Fychan ap Iorwerth ap Gwarin ap Caradog ap Ynyr Fychan.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane ferch Hywel Or Howell x(은)는 대략 1270년 웨일스 앤글레시에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Hywel ap Iorwerth Fychan ap Iorwerth ap Gwarin ap Caradog ap Ynyr Fychan(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane ferch Hywel Or Howell x nasceu aproximadamente 1270, em Anglesey, Gales. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas com Hywel ap Iorwerth Fychan ap Iorwerth ap Gwarin ap Caradog ap Ynyr Fychan.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane ferch Hywel Or Howell x родилась около 1270 г. в местности англеси, Уэльс. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери от Hywel ap Iorwerth Fychan ap Iorwerth ap Gwarin ap Caradog ap Ynyr Fychan.

es.lifeSummary: Jane ferch Hywel Or Howell x nació aproximadamente 1270, en Isla Oscura, Gales. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas con Hywel ap Iorwerth Fychan ap Iorwerth ap Gwarin ap Caradog ap Ynyr Fychan.
